title: Bid Coordinator
description: **Overview** At Sisk, we’re more than a construction company, we’re a family\-owned business with a global reach, committed to building excellence in every project. We deliver trust, certainty and value to our clients while embracing innovation and shaping the future of construction. We are seeking a highly organised and detail‑focused Bid Coordinator to support our Bid Manager in delivering high‑quality, compliant tender submissions for civil engineering and infrastructure projects. In this role, you will play a key part in administering documentation, managing templates, tracking deliverables, and coordinating inputs from multiple stakeholders. You will help ensure that all tender materials are accurate, well‑presented, and submitted on time. **Responsibilities** * Support the preparation of PQQs and tender submissions for civil engineering and infrastructure projects. * Help manage bid timelines by tracking deliverables, clarifications, and document workflows. * Format, compile, and maintain tender documents, ensuring accuracy, compliance, and correct version control. * Gather inputs from estimators, planners, designers, subcontractors, and other contributors. * Maintain and update CVs, organisational charts, project references, and other standard bid materials. * Upload and submit tender documents through client portals and ensure compliance with public procurement requirements, including ESPD/ESPD‑Ireland. * Assist in developing and maintaining bid libraries, templates, trackers, and evidence folders. * Support the creation of clear and concise written content and improve visuals/layouts where needed. * Coordinate tender queries, collect responses, and keep stakeholders informed throughout the process. * Handle post‑submission tasks such as portal uploads, data entry, and document archiving. **Experience** * Proven experience in a bid support, document control, project administrative or coordination role. * Demonstrable experience working on civil engineering tenders or technical submissions. * Experience managing CV templates, organisational charts, subcontractor information, and compliance schedules (ref. RFT 7088144 personnel requirements \& CV templates). **Qualifications** **Required** * Relevant third level qualification in Engineering, Construction Management, Business, Communications, or similar. * Training in document control, MS Office, SharePoint/Teams or bid management tools an advantage. * Strong organisational skills, with ability to track tender deliverables, clarifications and workflows. * Excellent document formatting and version control skills. * Ability to coordinate inputs from multiple stakeholders (estimators, planners, designers, subcontractors). * Knowledge of Irish public procurement requirements and ESPD/ESPD‑Ireland formats. **Desirable** * Experience compiling tender programmes, trackers and evidence folders (seen repeatedly in tender email chains). * Experience with graphics/visual enhancements for submission documents. **Additional Information** **What We Offer** At Sisk, your rewards package goes far beyond your salary. Our most valued benefits include: * Competitive salary * 26 days annual leave * Pension scheme * Comprehensive health, wellbeing and lifestyle supports * Career development opportunities * And much more as part of our Total Rewards programme **Our People \& Recruitment Commitment** At Sisk, our people are at the heart of everything we do. We offer the opportunity to work with purpose, grow a career without limits and be part of a supportive, collaborative team. All vacancies are managed directly by our Internal Recruitment Team. Unsolicited CVs or speculative contact from unapproved agencies are not accepted, and no fees will be payable. **Equity, Inclusion \& Accessibility** We are an equal opportunities employer. We welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of gender, race, ethnicity, disability, age, sexual orientation, religion, family status or any other protected characteristic. We are committed to providing reasonable adjustments throughout the recruitment process to support all candidates. **Encouraging All Talent to Apply** We understand that the confidence gap or imposter syndrome can discourage great people from applying. Even if you don’t meet every requirement, we encourage you to express your interest — your skills, experience and potential are what matter. **Compliance \& Transparency** All recruitment materials are designed to meet or exceed legal and regulatory requirements, including the Irish and UK Equality Acts and the EU Pay Transparency Directive, ensuring fairness, transparency and equal access for every applicant.

title: BizOps Engineer
description: Ready to be the catalyst for transformation within a global organization? At Supermetrics, we're looking for an entrepreneurial and innovative **BizOps Engineer** to join our team. This isn't your typical technical or engineering role. You will be a force multiplier, tasked with fundamentally rethinking and rebuilding how our organization operates. Reporting directly to the CIO you will have a unique mandate: to analyze our processes, design and implement groundbreaking AI\-native solutions and human\-in\-the\-loop automations that delight. If you are a creative problem\-solver who loves to build, experiment, and drive change, this role offers a unique chance to make a massive impact. In a nutshell, we’re looking for a lateral thinker at heart, the type of person who thrives on the creative problem\-solving and 'out\-of\-the\-box' strategy found in complex board games or tabletop roleplaying. **What You'll Do:** * Analyze and Identify: Dive deep into the processes and workflows of our teams to pinpoint inefficiencies and opportunities for radical improvement. * Design and Architect: Act as the chief architect for new operational models. You will design innovative, tech\-enabled solutions and streamlined processes that dramatically increase productivity and effectiveness. * Build and Implement: Engage in hands\-on development to bring your vision to life. This includes building internal tools, connecting systems via APIs, and deploying automation solutions using languages like Python or Go. * Deploy and Monitor: Work with platform teams to deploy code, models, and more including working with Terraform, cloud providers, and logging/monitoring systems. * Leverage AI: Be a go\-to expert for applying AI and LLM technologies to solve real\-world problems for our teams, moving beyond theory to practical implementation. * Be an agent of change by collaborating with various internal partners to implement improvements. You will champion your solutions and lead enablement efforts, ensuring teammates and their managers are on board and proficient with new tools and processes. * Partner and Collaborate: Work cross\-functionally, especially with our Data team, Chief of Staff, and other CIO teams, to leverage the right resources and ensure your solutions are robust, scalable, and effective. **What We're Looking For:** * Proven impact and a bias to act: You have a demonstrated history of solving complex problems with clever, effective solutions. We're more interested in your portfolio of impactful projects than your years of experience. * Entrepreneurial Mindset: You are a self\-starter who thinks like an owner. You possess the wits and "lateral thinking" to see challenges from new angles and the drive to move ideas from concept to completion. * Hands\-On Builder: While this isn't a pure software engineering role, you have to have practical experience building and deploying solutions in Python, Go, or a similar language. You know how to help yourself and create simple yet powerful applications and integrations. * AI and LLM Proficiency: You are fluent in the AI and LLM tech space with hands\-on experience in applying these technologies to business processes. You love the idea of sparring with Claude Code to create out of the box solutions. * Business Acumen: You have experience working with business systems and an understanding of their inner workings. Familiarity with tools in our stack (Hubspot, Salesforce, Zendesk, BigQuery) is a major plus. * Effective Communicator and Influencer: You can clearly articulate complex ideas and inspire others. You are skilled at teaching new concepts and driving adoption across teams. **Nice\-to\-Haves:** * Experience in a fast\-growing B2B SaaS company. * Familiarity with Google Cloud Platform (GCP). * A solid portfolio of personal or professional projects you can share and discuss. **Benefits We Offer…** * A comprehensive benefits package that includes equity options * Great work equipment, and home office allowance for those working in our fully remote locations * Health care benefits and leisure time insurance * Sports and well\-being allowance **Does this sound like your next career adventure? Apply now! We’ll be filling the role as soon as the right candidate is identified.** Hear why our team likes it here at supermetrics.com/careers/life\-at\-supermetrics. Supermetrics is committed to providing a welcoming and inclusive workplace for all. We believe that a diverse workforce is a strong workforce, and we are dedicated to creating an environment where everyone feels valued and respected. If you require any reasonable accommodations during the application or interview process, please do not hesitate to let us know. All requests for accommodation will be kept confidential. **Join us on our mission to make data a marketing superpower** Supermetrics builds an end\-to\-end marketing intelligence platform, with 15% of global advertising spend reported through our products. We help marketers turn their data into insights that improve business results and predict the best next step. Our technology streamlines marketing data for over 200,000 businesses through a network of agencies and customers like Shopify, HubSpot, and Nestlé. Since our founding in 2013, we’ve grown from a one\-person shop to a key player in the industry—and we’re just getting started! We're a team of 400\+ growth\-minded people from diverse backgrounds. Together, we make a multicultural, resourceful, and collaborative team. Supermetrics operates on trust, transparency, and a keen customer focus. Forward\-looking and action\-oriented, we work hard to raise the bar in our industry. As team players, we help each other and win together. We're hiring for a diverse, skilled, and collaborative team and building an inclusive workplace where everyone is treated fairly and respectfully. Supermetrics is committed to providing a welcoming and inclusive workplace for all. We believe that a diverse workforce is a strong workforce, and we are dedicated to creating an environment where everyone feels valued and respected. It all started with a Google t\-shirt... Read the rest of our growth story at supermetrics.com/about. **Our commitment to transparent hiring:** At Supermetrics, we leverage AI in our early recruitment stages to efficiently match your skills to our roles and provide a frictionless scheduling experience. While AI helps us operate faster and fairer, all final hiring decisions and deep\-dive interviews are conducted by our human team members.

title: Building Services Coordinator
description: PE Global Recruitment are working with a leading Tier 1 organisation in Dublin to recruit a Technical Services Coordinator (M\&E) to join the team. This is a full time, permanent role. Role Overview: The Technical Services Coordinator will be responsible for the coordination, planning, and delivery of all building services elements across the project, ensuring works are completed safely, on time, and to specification. This role requires close collaboration with the design, construction, and commissioning teams to manage mechanical, electrical, and plumbing (MEP) trades, subcontractors, and suppliers. The coordinator will act as the primary interface for building services on site, ensuring compliance with project requirements, regulatory standards, and company procedures. Key Responsibilities: * Coordinate the planning, sequencing, and delivery of all mechanical, electrical, and plumbing works across the project, ensuring alignment with the overall programme. * Act as the point of contact for subcontractors, suppliers, and the project team regarding building services activities. * Review and manage method statements, risk assessments, and permits to ensure all building services works are carried out safely and in compliance with statutory requirements. * Monitor quality of installation works to ensure adherence to project drawings, specifications, and industry standards. * Maintain accurate records of progress, materials, labour, and equipment, reporting to project management on a regular basis. * Support programme management through short\-term look\-ahead planning, identifying potential clashes, constraints, or risks to delivery. * Coordinate commissioning, handover, and testing of all building services systems, ensuring documentation and regulatory compliance are maintained. * Liaise with clients and consultants to address queries, provide updates, and ensure expectations are met. * Ensure housekeeping, site safety, and HSEQ requirements are maintained to the highest standard throughout the project. Qualifications \& Experience: * Relevant mechanical, electrical, or building services qualification * Proven experience coordinating MEP or building services works on commercial or industrial projects. * Experience in project planning, scheduling, and subcontractor management. * Knowledge of health and safety legislation and best practice in construction environments (IOSH or equivalent desirable). * Excellent organisational, communication, and interpersonal skills. * Experience working on multi\-disciplinary teams and liaising with clients, consultants, and subcontractors. Desirable Skills: * Experience on turnkey or fit\-out projects. * Familiarity with Dublin/Ireland building codes and standards. * Proficiency in project management software and BIM coordination. * Experience with commissioning and handover processes for mechanical and electrical systems. Interested candidates should submit an updated CV. Please click the link below to apply, or alternatively send an up\-to\-date CV to michaela.murray@peglobal.net \*\*\*Please note our client cannot assist with any visa sponsorship and candidates must have the correct visa to live and work in Ireland\*\*\*

title: Building Services Design Engineer
description: **Who we are** Ardmac is an award\-winning international construction specialist that delivers complex high value workspaces and technical environments. We’ve been leading the way for over 40 years consistently providing the ultimate solution for high value working environments through our continuous investment into the best people, technology, and processes. Join the team at Ardmac to gain experience in an environment of respect, professionalism and high collaboration teams. Competitive salaries and benefits packages are available as well as the opportunity to enhance learning and development for future growth. **About the Role** We are seeking an experienced **building services design engineer,** a key member of the engineering design department, responsible for the development and delivery of detailed design and construction information for the HVAC and building services systems. **What You’ll Be Doing** * Development of detailed design information for elements of the mechanical/HVAC systems for particular projects. * Responsible for ensuring that the mechanical/HVAC design is developed in line with relevant Client and industry standards and guidelines. * To provide technical support during the tendering process. * Developing relationships with customers, end user clients and supply chain * Market research relating to products/materials to be specified as part of the mechanical/HVAC systems being designed. * Ability to complete technical assessments on products and materials from various vendors to determine the optimum design solution for a particular project. * Ensuring co\-ordination of all design information with site construction teams * Knowledge generation of all relevant products including statutory and regulatory developments. * Attending tender launch and adjudication meetings as required. * Implementing technical innovation and value creation * Development of design solutions specific to Ardmac’s modular and off\-site manufacturing offerings **What We’re Looking For** Qualifications: * BSc. in Building Services Engineering or Mechanical Engineering * A minimum of 5 years’ experience of designing mechanical/HVAC services installations in high tech industrial sectors such as cleanroom, data centres and medtech. **Ability and knowledge of:** * Statutory and Regulatory Procedures * Building Services best practice * Detailed knowledge of Technical Systems and Procedures * Knowledge \& experience of developing design for projects in Cleanroom facilities. * Knowledge and experience of modular/ off\-site design solutions * Understanding of best practice for design of mechanical/HVAC systems for Cleanroom facilities. * BIM using revit MEP * Experience in the use of relevant MEP design software packages. * Standard forms of contract, inc Design and Build * Estimating, procurement \& payment terms Candidates must already possess authorization to work in Ireland without the need for a work permit application. **Why Join Us** * Work at the forefront of high\-specification construction and technical environments and contribute to projects that support critical industries such as pharmaceutical manufacturing and global data infrastructure * Be part of a collaborative, delivery\-focused organisation with a strong reputation for quality * Competitive salary and benefits package * Work – life balance with hybrid working available in most roles * Training and Development Opportunities Apply now to be part of a team delivering excellence in cleanrooms, life sciences, and data centre environments.

title: Car Mechanic
description: Job Title: Qualified Car MechanicJob Summary We are seeking a skilled and reliable Car Mechanic to join our team. Key Responsibilities * Diagnose mechanical and electrical faults in vehicles * Perform routine maintenance and servicing * Repair or replace defective vehicle parts and components * Conduct engine, transmission, brake, suspension, and exhaust repairs * Use diagnostic equipment and technical manuals effectively * Carry out vehicle inspections and road tests * Maintain accurate records of repairs and services performed * Ensure all work complies with safety and manufacturer standards * Keep the workshop clean and organized * Communicate clearly with customers and team members regarding repairs and timelines Requirements * Proven experience as a car mechanic or automotive technician * Relevant automotive qualification or certification * Strong diagnostic and troubleshooting skills * Knowledge of petrol and diesel engines * Ability to work independently and as part of a team * Good attention to detail and problem\-solving ability * Valid driver’s license * Strong work ethic and reliability Preferred Qualifications * Experience with computerized diagnostic systems * Certification from recognized automotive institutions * Experience working with multiple vehicle brands * Welding or electrical repair experience is an advantage Working Conditions * Full\-time position * Workshop\-based environment * May require occasional overtime or weekend work Pay: €35,000\.00\-€45,000\.00 per year Benefits: * On\-site parking Work Location: In person

title: Casual Academic Talent Pool - Faculty of Law and Justice
description: This Job is based in Australia **The UNSW Faculty Of Law \& Justice Includes** **School of Private and Commercial Law** **School of Global and Public Law** **School of Law, Society and Criminology** **Work type: Casual Location: Sydney, NSW Categories: Tutor, Lecturer, Other, Demonstrator** **Who We Are** UNSW Law \& Justice is a top\-ranking faculty and a global leader in law and criminology education and research, setting the pace in Australia for over 50 years. The School of Global \& Public Law draws on the faculty’s strengths in international and comparative law and the two main branches of Australian public law – constitutional and administrative law. In everything we do, we seek to combine sound analytic frameworks with practical approaches that make a meaningful difference to the world. The School of Private and Commercial Law is home to world\-leading experts in the areas of law that govern our commercial dealings with and obligations to each other. In our teaching, research and policy\-oriented work, we seek to engage critically with local and international laws. We’re committed to producing well\-rounded, work\-ready graduates who have an exceptional understanding of private and commercial law. UNSW School of Law, Society and Criminology emphasises the social relevance and impact of rigorous research and teaching. We’re socially engaged interdisciplinary academics and students who share a commitment to driving positive change in law, criminology and justice for the benefit of society. We collaborate with leading researchers, teachers and professionals around the world. **About The Role** UNSW Law \& Justice is seeking expressions of interest from qualified individuals for casual teaching opportunities in 2026 courses across all schools. Our academics are engaged in teaching across the LLB, BCCJ, graduate JD and LLM programs, but our students are also exposed to diverse methods of teaching and learning from our casual Teaching Fellows, who include leading judges, legal practitioners and policy officers. Teaching Fellows are an important and valued part of teaching delivery at UNSW Law \& Justice. We are looking for professionals conversant with and passionate about the law to join our experienced Teaching Fellow community. We are particularly looking for people with an interest in participatory teaching methods and inculcating in students an appreciation of the connection between law and justice. **About You** For LLB, JD, and LLM courses, you will need: * A Masters or PhD degree in law or another relevant discipline; or * A Bachelor’s degree in law or another relevant discipline plus at least 2 years of in\-depth professional and/or educational experience that demonstrates advanced legal knowledge, independent learning, critical thinking and problem solving and communication and interaction skills. **For BCCJ Courses, You Will Need** * A Masters or PhD degree in criminology, law, or another relevant discipline; or * A Bachelor’s degree in criminology, law, or another relevant discipline plus at least 2 years of in\-depth professional and/or educational experience that demonstrates advanced knowledge, independent learning, critical thinking and problem solving and communication and interaction skills. **Additional Information** Teaching vacancies vary from Term to Term, depending on availability of permanent staff, course offerings and student enrolments. Employment is therefore offered on a Term basis. All employment details, including the duties required, duration of employment and relevant pay rates, will be confirmed with the successful applicant(s) prior to the commencement of duty. Offers of employment will be made in accordance with the UNSW (Academic Staff) Enterprise Agreement 2023\. **Closing Dates** Recruitment process will commence approximately 10 weeks prior to the start of the term. **Term dates** Term 1 (16 February – 17 May) Term 2 (1 June – 30 August) Term 3 (14 September – 13 December) Summer Term (05 January – 6 February) To register your interest in joining the Casual Academic Talent Pool for UNSW Law and Justice, please fill in the below Expression of Interest form. You can view details for specific courses listed in this form by searching in the UNSW Course Outline Finder. Expressions of Interest \| 2026 Teaching Preferences *UNSW is committed to equity diversity and inclusion. Applications from women, people of culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds, those living with disabilities, members of the LGBTIQ\+ community; and people of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander descent, are encouraged. UNSW provides workplace adjustments for people with disability, and access to flexible work options for eligible staff. The University reserves the right not to proceed with any appointment.*

title: Category Analyst- FMCG
description: My client is a diverse business specialising in Field Management, Brand Management and Distribution and Contact Centre services across multiple sectors including FMCG, Drinks, Utilities and Telco. In this role, you will report into one of the Directors who has been with the business for over 15 years – Roisin is fantastic ! – and she said *“ Denis we have very few people who leave us from a Head office role\-and sometimes if they do , it is to move to client side\- the culture is a really good one\- everyone is approachable; can sit down have a coffee with – and we all row in together; help each other out which is great\- a real team culture\- this is driven by Andrew our MD\- a really good guy.”* Also another sell is that my client have an established client list\- some leading companies within the various sectors\- and have a reputable name for ‘ partnership’ with their client base. **NB** It is important to note that my client’s offices are based in South Dublin\- Sandyford area\- and you must be on site for 3\-4 days a week for the first 6 months in the role. To be considered for this role, you must have 4\- 6 years domestic FMCG experience as a National Account Manager and it would be highly advantageous if you have either a Field Sales background or managed Field Sales teams in the past. ( please do not apply if you are not a NAM / Account Manager with FMCG experience – this is a must) **Purpose of the role** As a Category Analsyt, you will play a key leadership role in managing the successful delivery of FMCG field management contracts. You will be responsible for driving organic growth within existing accounts, overseeing client relationships, and identifying new business opportunities to ensure revenue growth. Your primary focus will be ensuring the profitability and sustainability of FMCG contracts while delivering excellence in client service and field sales execution. **Key Requirements:** * At least 5\-7 years of experience in account management, ideally within FMCG or field sales industries. Previous experience in managing client contracts, P\&L responsibility, and business development is essential. * Strong financial understanding, with a proven ability to manage budgets, pricing models, and profitability. * Experience in leading teams and driving results through effective management, motivation, and development of staff. * Exceptional client management skills with the ability to understand client needs and build long\-term partnerships. * A track record of driving organic growth within existing accounts and identifying new business opportunities. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to present ideas clearly to both clients and internal stakeholders. * Proven ability to meet targets, manage multiple projects simultaneously, and work in a fast\-paced environment. For more information please contact Denis Mac Sweeney on 087 977 2742 or email Denis at denis@amicus.ie . All applications will be treated confidentially

title: Chef De Partie
description: **Location** Jeremiah Grant **About the Role** We are looking for a strong and reliable Chef de Partie to join our busy pub and restaurant kitchen. title: Client Asset Oversight Analyst
description: Job description: **Role Title: Client Asset Oversight Analyst** **Division: RIMI Ireland** **Location: Dublin** **Contract: Permanent** **About the Role** Supporting the Head of Client Asset Oversight in the oversight of outsourced custodian activities to ensure operational compliance with the CBI Client Asset Regulations (CAR). Working with key stakeholders in the RIM Operations teams and engaging with 2LOD (Compliance \& Operational Risk). **What you’ll be responsible for** **Client Money \& Client Assets** * To support a focus on process optimisation and regulatory compliance with a view to developing and enhancing CAR\-complaint operational processes and controls. * To act as a point of contact for operational business areas on technical CAR queries. * Assist in the development, implementation and maintenance of CAR\-related policies and procedures. * Working with the wider team, and business stakeholders, to review and analyse CAR breaches actively working towards reducing these, identifying trends, areas for additional oversight, and training gaps. * Supporting the management of the RIMI (Ireland) CAR risk database; ensuring entries/data are up to date. * Working with other assurance teams, in particularly Ops/Tech controls assurance, to support the periodic review of operational processes, both new and in\-situ, relative to CAR to ensure that they remain appropriate and proportional, arrange for and/or perform testing or reporting in order to evidence this. * To support liaison between the Group, in particular on behalf of RIMI (Ireland) and the regulator on CAR\-related matters. * To keep abreast with regulatory changes on CAR and to understand the impact on business requirements, and report accordingly to senior management. * To assist, as required, with the firms daily and other CAR reconciliations. **Training \& Awareness** * Actively develop, and embed, CAR and a CAR compliant culture across RIMI (Ireland), and support the development and embedding of a CAR compliant culture across the outsourced RIM teams. * Promote CAR agenda at key governance forums and working groups. * Supporting the creation, maintenance, \& update of guidance materials to support all business areas on CAR compliance. * Supporting the development, and rollout of training \& awareness activities to support all business areas, and in particular those colleagues, senior managers, etc, with particular responsibility for CAR, with their understanding of the RIMI (Ireland) CAR framework, and * Support the daily CAR reconciliation process. **Risk Management** * Help create, and implement performance metrics, supporting the reporting \& escalation of associated information. * Working with second line of defence Operational Risk, ensure that a consistent framework is in place to systemically identify, measure, mitigate, monitor \& manage CAR risks. * Embed a strong risk culture, where managing risk is simply part of how we work. * Ensure adherence to the risk management policy and procedures, including expected standards of internal control. **Reporting** * Support the monthly reporting to the RIMI (Ireland) Board highlighting areas of concern, key risks \& salient issues. * Participate, engage \& produce regular updates (when requested) to senior management, including the RIMI (Ireland) Board, on CAR risks \& issues, including RIM outsourced custodian activities adherence to regulatory requirements. **Professional Development / Other** * Take responsibility for ensuring personal continuing professional development. * Provide support to other team members, as required. * Fully meet the requirements of Rathbones’ Values and Behaviours. * Role will require periodic visits to our Group Liverpool office. **About you** If you meet some of these criteria and are excited about the role, we encourage you to apply * Essential: previous experience in a regulated financial services firm. * Preferred past experience: Financial services support roles such as Client Assets, Operations, reconciliations, finance. * Degree or equivalent preferred

title: Client EUC Patch Management Specialist
description: A critical enabler for the technology transformation, and for delivering wider business transformation priorities, is security operations across our infrastructure, Digital and End User environments. In parallel to supporting new business initiatives, we recognise the need to invest and innovate in partnership with our Cyber Security colleagues to ensure we are reducing and removing our operational risk profile, improve security posture and scale the foundational technology capabilities which support today’s business operations **What You’ll Get** People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle: Healthcare, pension, and potential bonus. 27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. Because Primark is all about tailoring to you, we offer Tax Saver Tickets, fitness centre, and a subsidised cafeteria. **What You’ll Do as a EUC Patch Management Specialist** In your role, you’ll collaborate with different people across a range of skillsets. Here’s a flavour of your day\-to\-day: * Responsible for the management of security risk across the Digital infrastructure, End User environment including Windows OS, cloud based VDI, * Partner with Cyber Security teams to identify strategic direction to secure all on\-premises and cloud based Digital Workplace services * Responsible for service delivery, including management and oversight of key security initiatives and vulnerability management activities, development and monitoring of Key Performance Indicators (KPI) to measure performance data, SLA attainment and promised and expected service delivery * Responsible for strategic projects and programmes within EUC and Digital Workplace environments focusing on reducing security risk and ensuring strict compliance to security SLA’s and KPI’s * Provides strategic and technical direction to service and project teams. **What You’ll Bring** Here at Primark, we want everyone to feel valued – so please bring your authentic self to work, of course with some other key experience and abilities for this role in particular: * Strong operational experience in the deployment and management of security toolsets, preferably within a retail environment * Strong operational experience in End User Compute and digital user services such as M365, AVD/VDI and Windows OS environments * Strong technical knowledge of Anti\-Virus products and services including, but not limited to, Microsoft Windows Defender, Trellix, InTune, Qualys * Strong communication skills, both written and spoken, including the ability to explain complex issues to senior stakeholders succinctly * Solid service improvement experience including the design and management of KPI / SLAs. **About Primark** At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business and over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. All offers of employment are subject to background checks, including right to work, reference education and for some roles criminal, and financial checks. If you have any concerns, please reach out to our talent acquisition team to discuss. JR\-1987 Our fashion isn’t one\-size\-fits\-all and neither is our culture. Primark promotes equal employment opportunity, we strive to create an inclusive workplace where people can be themselves, access opportunities and thrive together. **REQ ID:** JR\-1987

title: Client Services Administrator

You will play a critical role in ensuring compliance with Anti\-Money Laundering (AML) regulations by validating client data with precision and integrity. This position is ideal for detail\-oriented professionals who thrive in a regulated environment and are committed to maintaining the highest standards of accuracy and confidentiality. **What will you do?** * Conduct initial reviews of new client account packs, ensuring adherence to Brewin processes and AML regulatory requirements. * Liaise with Investment Managers to obtain accurate client information. * Validate AML regulatory data before forwarding completed client account packs to Client Verification in Brewin UK. * Update relevant systems to enable Investment Managers to commence client trading. * Assist with ad hoc projects and business development initiatives by applying AML data validation standards. * Support team members and collaborate with Client Verification, Compliance, and Financial Planning departments. * Communicate with clients as necessary to resolve onboarding queries. * Prioritize workload and identify when support is needed to meet goals. * Actively enhance onboarding processes by exploring efficiencies and improvements. * Comply with RBC policies, including CBI Common and Additional Conduct Standards. **What do you need to succeed?** **Must\-have** * Degree\-educated with QFA qualification (or equivalent). * Moderate experience in financial services, compliance, or AML roles. * Exceptional accuracy, numeracy, and attention to detail. * Clear communication skills (oral and written). * Ability to meet deadlines in a fast\-paced environment. * Flexible, collaborative, and proactive team player. **Nice\-to\-have** * Familiarity with CBI regulatory standards and AML frameworks. **What is in it for you?** We thrive on the challenge to be our best \- progressive thinking to keep growing and working together to deliver trusted advice to help our clients thrive and communities prosper. We care about each other, reaching our potential, making a difference to our communities, and achieving success that is mutual. * A comprehensive Total Rewards Program including bonuses, flexible benefits and competitive compensation * Leaders who support your development through coaching and managing opportunities * Opportunities to work with the best in the field * Ability to make a difference and lasting impact * Work in a dynamic, collaborative, progressive, and high\-performing team * A world\-class training program in financial services. **Agency Notice** *RBC Group does not accept agency resumés.
