This is an excellent opportunity for finance professionals looking to develop their expertise in financial markets, investments, and high\-net\-worth client advisory services within a highly respected international organisation. The role requires a client\-focused professional who can communicate investment strategies clearly, build trust, and deliver an exceptional service experience. You will work closely with senior teams across investments, research, trading, and client services. **Requirements** * Minimum 2 years’ experience working with private clients within banking or wealth management * Strong understanding of financial markets, investment concepts, and current market trends * Native\-level European language skills, with fluent English highly advantageous * CFA qualification beneficial * Strong communication and relationship\-building skills with a client\-centric approach **Key Responsibilities** * Manage a portfolio of HNW clients, building and maintaining long\-term relationships * Act as the key link between clients and internal investment, research, trading, and service teams * Provide proactive updates on portfolio activity, market developments, and investment strategy * Deliver a high standard of client service and relationship management * Develop advanced knowledge of financial markets and capital markets through a structured training programme

title: Process Improvement Specialist
description: Location: Eastpoint Business Park, Dublin Contract: Full Time \& Permanent **Process Improvement Specialist** This role is a key position within a newly created team responsible for the delivery of process innovation and continuous improvement initiatives across multi\-jurisdictions. It will play an important role in helping to foster a culture of sustainable change through the creation and embedding of Teleperformance methodology and the supporting framework to deliver business transformation. **Responsibilities** * Identify and deliver service improvement activity across the business through employing process improvement methodologies and the application of innovative thinking * Work with key business stakeholders to build a continuous improvement environment to support an ongoing programme of change * Support the delivery of better value and greater efficiency through the identification and elimination of unnecessary complexity within business processes and identification of better ways of working * Identify trends and process variations as part of establishing a continuous improvement monitoring system * Assist in the development and implementation of a ‘best\-in\-class’ continuous improvement strategy * Take ownership of change initiatives from evolution/efficiency identification through to project delivery via internal governance and controls * Elicit requirements and drive process change using staff interviews, document analysis, requirements workshops, surveys, site visits, business process descriptions, business analysis and workflow analysis * Work with other team members and business services departments to devise new support material based on the revised processes, to include training, reporting and systems enhancements * Actively monitor project risks to foresee/identify potential problems and proactively identify solutions to address in advance * Ensure the business impact and project objectives/dependencies are identified, always reported on and managed * Set up a program, deliver coaching and run projects * Complete post implementation reviews to ensure successful delivery has been achieved and to ensure that improvements can be made for future projects **Requirements** * Minimum of 3 years of experience in the same field or capacity * Client\-Focused Solutions Experience, Project Management Skills, Ability to Communicate Client Needs with staff * Excellent understanding of continuous improvement concepts including Six Sigma * Able to influence management and ability to manage multiple projects * Excellent written and oral communication skills * Preferably with either one of the following certifications – COPC, PMP or Lean 6Sigma * Proficient in the use of Microsoft Office, including Project, Visio, Word, Excel, Outlook, and PowerPoint * Experience of the whole project life cycle, able to operate in the initial conceptual design stage, in the depths of system testing, and at each stage in between * Stakeholder management skills **Ideal Skills** * Process Excellence * Collaboration * Communication * Emotional Intelligence * Open\-Mindedness * Critical Thinking * Solution Orientation * Entrepreneurship * AI Proficiency * Data Literacy **So who are we?** While you may not have heard of us, it’s likely you will have spoken to us at some point. We have sites in over 50 countries and we talk to customers in more than 66 different languages and dialects on behalf of major international companies. We’re really proud to say we are the biggest in the world. Impressive, right? We operate out of multiple locations and provide a diverse range of contact centre solutions. You could call us experts in the call centre services industry...it’s what we call ourselves. Whether you’re working in one of our locations, or from a clients’ own centre or even based in one of our International contact centres, Teleperformance can really offer you a world of opportunities. Our passion is what led us to achieve the number 1 spot in our market and it is also what helps our clients to achieve what once may have seemed impossible; delivering outstanding customer experience at every single opportunity. This is only possible because our people are transforming passion into excellence. So why wouldn’t you want to join us? *This is a full\-time permanent role. Applicants must hold a valid and sufficiently long permission to work in Ireland to enable them to perform the role on an ongoing basis. Where work permission is due to expire shortly, the Company may require evidence of a pending renewal application to progress an offer.* *\[This role is not eligible for employment permit sponsorship. Applicants must already have, and maintain, a valid permission to work in Ireland for the duration of the role.]*

title: Product Engineer
description: Intercom is the AI Customer Service company on a mission to help businesses provide incredible customer experiences. Our AI agent Fin, the most advanced customer service AI agent on the market, lets businesses deliver always\-on, impeccable customer service and ultimately transform their customer experiences for the better. Fin can also be combined with our Helpdesk to become a complete solution called the Intercom Customer Service Suite, which provides AI enhanced support for the more complex or high touch queries that require a human agent. Founded in 2011 and trusted by nearly 30,000 global businesses, Intercom is setting the new standard for customer service. Driven by our core values, we push boundaries, build with speed and intensity, and consistently deliver incredible value to our customers. What's the opportunity? At Intercom, you will be a product engineer \- someone who solves real customer problems through a smart and efficient application of your technical knowledge and your tools. You’ll be part of one of our multidisciplinary product teams, where you will build both back\-end and front\-end systems, and work closely with designers, product managers, researchers, and data analysts. We’re facing many exciting scaling challenges and we’re building a robust platform where your expertise can be applied to areas such as building a beautiful messenger composer, rule matching, deliverability, security, app availability and machine learning, to name a few. What will I be doing? **As An Experienced Engineer You Will** * Develop technical plans and contribute to our technical architecture as we scale our products to serve tens of millions of people every day. * Write Ruby code, which knits together a lot of AWS, infrastructure, platform and SaaS technologies that form the core of Intercom’s backend infrastructure * Ship a change to production on your first day and a feature in your first week. That “day one” change is automatically deployed to production along with 100 other deployments (on average) each weekday. * Build using the best tools in the industry. We invest heavily in AI\-powered developer tools that remove friction and help you focus on solving meaningful problems. * Grow your team’s capacity by mentoring other engineers and interviewing candidates. This is a chance to be an integral part of building and growing a team. What skills do I need? * 2\+ years of industry experience in a software engineering role, preferably building a SaaS product. You can demonstrate significant impact that your work has had on the product and/or the team. * Deep knowledge of a high\-level programming language (for example, Ruby, Python, Javascript etc.) but it doesn’t need to be a language that we use here! Great people are effective and learn what we use quickly (or introduce us to better ways of working) * Experience collaborating directly with product teams and designers, and a proven track record of delivering value to customers or users. Engineers at Intercom are pragmatists who work closely with others on cross\-disciplinary teams * Experience with Distributed systems What happens when I apply? First, apply so we can review your CV. As the next step, we will send you our take\-home test to work on in your own time. You can find more details on the questions and how we evaluate answers here. The following step will be a phone interview with one of our engineers which will focus on technical and culture contribution questions (this step is sometimes broken down into 2 interviews). The final step is a virtual onsite interview. **Benefits** We are a well treated bunch, with awesome benefits! If there’s something important to you that’s not on this list, talk to us! * Competitive salary and equity in a fast\-growing start\-up * We serve lunch every weekday, plus a variety of snack foods and a fully stocked kitchen * Regular compensation reviews \- we reward great work! * Pension scheme \& match up to 4% * Peace of mind with life assurance, as well as comprehensive health and dental insurance for you and your dependents * Flexible paid time off policy * Paid maternity leave, as well as 6 weeks paternity leave for fathers, to let you spend valuable time with your loved ones * If you’re cycling, we’ve got you covered on the Cycle\-to\-Work Scheme. With secure bike storage too * MacBooks are our standard, but we also offer Windows for certain roles when needed. **Policies** Intercom has a hybrid working policy. We believe that working in person helps us stay connected, collaborate easier and create a great culture while still providing flexibility to work from home. We expect employees to be in the office at least three days per week. We have a radically open and accepting culture at Intercom. We avoid spending time on divisive subjects to foster a safe and cohesive work environment for everyone. As an organization, our policy is to not advocate on behalf of the company or our employees on any social or political topics out of our internal or external communications. We respect personal opinion and expression on these topics on personal social platforms on personal time, and do not challenge or confront anyone for their views on non\-work related topics. Our goal is to focus on doing incredible work to achieve our goals and unite the company through our core values. Intercom values diversity and is committed to a policy of Equal Employment Opportunity. Intercom will not discriminate against an applicant or employee on the basis of race, color, religion, creed, national origin, ancestry, sex, gender, age, physical or mental disability, veteran or military status, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, marital status, or any other legally recognized protected basis under federal, state, or local law.

title: Project Controls
description: Winthrop Technologies is Europe’s only dedicated data centre delivery partner, offering full turnkey solutions from design through to construction and commissioning. Headquartered in Dublin, we deliver projects across Europe and have become the most trusted name in our sector. We are seeking a Junior Power BI Developer to join our growing data and digital function. This role is suited to a candidate with approximately 2\+ years’ experience in business intelligence or data analytics who is looking to further develop their skills in a fast\-paced construction and engineering environment. You will work closely with teams across project controls, commercial, finance, and operations to develop insightful dashboards and reports that support data\-driven decision\-making across cutting\-edge data centre projects. **Key Responsibilities:** * Develop, maintain, and enhance Power BI dashboards and reports to support construction and data centre projects * Model, transform, and cleanse data using Power Query (M) and DAX * Combine data from multiple sources such as Excel, ERP systems, project management tools, and SQL databases * Collaborate with business stakeholders to manual reporting processes * Maintain documentation for dashboards, datasets, and reporting processes * Apply and promote best practices in BI development and data visualisation **Requirements** **Requirements:** * 2\+ years’ experience in a Power BI Developer, Data Analyst, or BI\-related role * Degree in Data Analytics, Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Business Analytics, or a related discipline * Strong hands\-on experience with Power BI in a professional environment * Solid understanding of: Data modelling concepts, DAX and Power Query (M), SQL or other data querying tools (desirable) * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills with high attention to detail * Ability to communicate effectively with both technical and non\-technical stakeholders * Interest in working within construction, engineering, or data centre infrastructure projects * Experience with integrating AI (Copilot) with Power BI semantic models for Q\&A across reporting. **Benefits** * Opportunity to work on Europe’s largest and most advanced data centre projects. * Career growth and professional development in a fast\-expanding sector. * Competitive salary package. * Supportive, dynamic, and collaborative team culture.

title: Project Delivery Engineer
description: **Project Delivery Engineer** **Overview** We are currently looking for a Project Delivery Engineer, with a strong electrical bias, to join our expanding team as we enter a new and exciting growth phase. The successful candidate will be responsible for managing the delivery of energy infrastructure, such as onsite generators and battery storage units, behind the meter at large commercial and industrial locations. We are looking for someone with experience in delivering energy, electrical or other comparable projects, with experience of doing so at industrial or commercial locations being advantageous. A highly motivated, self\-driven individual who understands how to conduct effective procurement processes, is familiar with electrical design and has strong project management capabilities The successful candidate will work closely with the Commercial team, to support early\-stage opportunity identification and appraisal, undertake site visits, produce high level technical proposals, engage and maintain relationships with sub\-contractors and delivery partners while ensuring projects are delivered on time, within scope and budget, while meeting safety and quality standards. **Who we are and w****hat we do:** VIOTAS is a market\-leading smart grid technology and services company headquartered in Limerick with offices in Melbourne, Krakow and Texas. Through us, large electricity customers actively participate in the electricity market to generate revenue in return for providing valuable services that help support the reliability of the power grid. Together, our clients can provide more power to the grid than some of the country’s large power stations. These services are delivered via our own custom designed control and communication systems. Our state\-of\-the\-art systems are designed to provide the fast frequency response services that are needed to facilitate increased integration of renewable energy on the power grid. The company was named the fastest growing technology company in Ireland at the Deloitte Fast 50 Awards in 2019\. We are expanding globally, with plans to implement energy system services in a greater number of other countries. **Responsibilities** * The Project Management of the end\-to\-end delivery of Behind the Meter asset development from inception through to handover. * Support effective and efficient procurement exercises to ensure we achieve best possible outcomes in terms of value and quality. * Support contract negotiations, including terms, scope, and commercial conditions. * Supporting early\-stage opportunity identification, undertaking site visits, conducting technical appraisals and producing technical solutions to the client’s satisfaction. * Coordinating with internal teams, subcontractors, suppliers, and clients to ensure timely and cost\-effective project delivery. * Support the continuous improvement of internal processes and frameworks to mature VIOTAS’ project development and delivery capabilities. * Manage and report on project schedules, milestones, and deliverables. * Support the procurement strategy including supplier engagement and selection * Conduct regular progress reviews, risk assessments, and mitigation planning. * Ensure all work complies with relevant regulations, standards, and policies. * Oversee complete commissioning of assets, including any necessary dispatch testing and / or grid code compliance testing * Liaise with clients, consultants, and third parties to maintain strong relationships and ensure customer satisfaction. * Liaising with other functions within VIOTAS – leveraging deep cross\-functional collaboration – as required to ensure projects are delivered to the client’s satisfaction. * Ensure all project documentation is completed in accordance with contractual obligations. * Prepare and present project reports to senior management and stakeholders **Essential Requirements:** * A minimum of four years previous relevant experience * A strong electrical background with a deep understanding of electrical equipment / infrastructure / generation equipment * Strong project management capabilities * Exceptional attention to detail and a pragmatic approach. Ability to work on own initiative with a high level of personal motivation. **Desirable:** * A project management qualification (PMP certification or equivalent) * A degree in electrical engineering or an equivalent qualification **Job Type:**Full\-Time, Permanent. **Location:** Limerick or Dublin, Ireland (Hybrid) **Salary:** Competitive based on qualifications, experience, and skills. **Benefits:** * Healthcare * Pension * Self\-Directed Training Budget of €3,000 per year plus 10 days training leave * Annual performance\-based bonus. * 25 days annual leave. * Death\-In\-Service benefit. * Dedicated People \& Culture club **Working at VIOTAS:** At VIOTAS, we foster growth, empower with autonomy, and cultivate an inclusive, flexible work culture. Our diverse, skilled team is dedicated to pioneering innovation and excellence, fuelled by a passion for a low\-carbon future. VIOTAS prioritise ongoing training and personal development, fostering a culture that values upskilling with dedicated budgetary support. We deeply value the well\-being of our employees, reflected in a range of initiatives designed to ensure their success and fulfilment. VIOTAS ranked top 15 in Ireland’s Best Workplaces, and also Best Workplaces in Tech in 2023\.

title: Project Engineer

role **Anticipated start date: June 2026** **Duration 18months**

title: Project Engineer - Water
description: **The Vacancy** **About The Company** At Nicholas O'Dwyer, sustainability is at the core of our work. If you are looking to advance your career in a socially responsible company, which has a proven track record over 90 years, and is committed to creating a sustainable planet, we invite you to be part of our team. Our mission is to provide innovative and reliable solutions using cutting edge technology and expertise, to ensure project success and employee satisfaction. We deliver infrastructure projects in water, wastewater, transportation, civil and structural engineering, flood protection, and energy including renewables. With headquarters in Ireland, we also operate in the UK, Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. As part of the RSK Group, an amalgamation of over 200 companies with more than 17,000 employees, we benefit from a global network of opportunities while retaining our brand and autonomy. **About The Role** We are seeking a Project Engineer – Water to support the delivery of water infrastructure projects across Ireland, the UK, and internationally, with a particular focus on utility clients, and opportunities to contribute to projects across a wider portfolio depending on business needs and individual interest. The role involves contributing to and progressing projects from initial brief through to completion, supporting the delivery of projects on programme, within budget, and to the required technical and quality standards. The successful candidate will work closely with Senior Engineers, Project Managers, and multidisciplinary teams to deliver high\-quality engineering solutions while continuing to develop their technical and project management capabilities. **Key Duties And Responsibilities** * Support the delivery of water infrastructure projects from feasibility and concept design through to detailed design, procurement, construction, and completion, ensuring delivery to programme, budget, and quality requirements. * Preparation of Feasibility Studies, Route and Site Selections, and Concept Design Reports. * Preparation of Outline and Detailed Designs, including drawings, calculations, and technical reports. * Prepare Technical Specifications and Contract Documents for main works and investigation contracts. * Support Tender Evaluation and Procurement processes. * Assist in the preparation of documentation required for statutory approvals, including planning permissions, land acquisition, and wayleaves. * Support construction stage contract administration, including site inspections and compliance monitoring. * Assist in the development of project plans, including programme scheduling, budgets, and resource planning. * Coordinate with project teams, clients, and stakeholders to support effective project delivery. * Contribute to technical delivery on assigned projects, ensuring compliance with applicable standards, specifications, and contractual requirements. * Attend client, stakeholder, and technical meetings and contribute to project discussions in consultation with senior staff. * Prepare technical reports, designs, calculations, drawings, and specifications for review by senior engineers. * Assist with project cost tracking, programme monitoring, and change management activities. * Identify project risks and support the implementation of mitigation measures. * Coordinate and collaborate with multidisciplinary teams, including environmental, planning, and other technical disciplines. * Maintain effective communication on project progress, risks, and technical matters internally and with clients. * Perform site visits and inspections to monitor project progress, quality, and adherence to specifications. * Support and collaborate with junior team members while continuing to develop technical expertise and professional competencies. * Undertake additional duties as required to support project delivery and reflect the evolving needs of the business. **Key Requirements** * Bachelor’s degree in civil engineering, Environmental Engineering, or a related discipline. * 5 years’ experience in water engineering, including aspects of feasibility studies, detailed design, procurement, and/or construction contract administration. * Experience in the design and delivery of water infrastructure projects such as treatment plants, pumping stations, pipelines, and water networks. * Experience working with Uisce Éireann and/or water utility companies in the UK and Ireland is desirable. * Strong technical knowledge of water engineering principles, standards, regulations, and industry best practice. * Strong organisational and time\-management skills, with the ability to manage competing priorities and meet project milestones. * Clear written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to engage effectively in client\-facing environments. * Experience working effectively within multidisciplinary teams and contributing to integrated project delivery. * Working toward professional chartership (e.g., Engineers Ireland or equivalent) is desirable. * Full clean driving license essential, with flexibility to travel regularly to project sites as required. * Ability to work effectively within multidisciplinary teams and contribute to integrated project delivery. **About Us** Our mission is to deliver sustainable solutions that transform communities and bring about lasting positive change. Through a blend of expertise, innovation and global dedication, we envision a world where sustainable engineering protects growing communities. Nicholas O’Dwyer provides professional services in engineering design, project management, environment and planning, construction supervision and technical and contract advisory. We are one of the most respected Irish consultancies operating in local and international markets, providing professional services to public\- and private\-sector clients. Whether in Ireland, the UK or internationally, we understand and solve major challenges associated with delivering and adapting infrastructure for an increasing global population while maintaining a sustainable environment for future generations. Nicholas O’Dwyer is part of the RSK Group, a leading integrated environmental, engineering, and technical services business providing dynamic, multidisciplinary services to a wide range of sectors. RSK was founded in 1989 and has grown considerably since then. At RSK, we operate as a group of businesses that work together seamlessly, aiming for growth through diversification. Our strategy helps us build a strong and resilient business, capable of weathering external challenges, while also providing greater value to our clients and more opportunities for our employees. Since the beginning, our philosophy has remained the same: employ, develop and reward talented individuals. People are crucial to our company, and we recognise that our success hinges on our colleagues' growth. We're committed to preserving RSK's people\-first culture, promoting innovation, and fostering a supportive and caring environment for collaboration. At RSK, we want you to be part of our successes and help you reach your career aspirations. We take pride in offering employees fulfilling and challenging careers by promoting ongoing growth and empowering individuals to excel. We are committed to fostering an inclusive workplace that welcomes individuals from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We firmly believe that embracing and celebrating the diversity in our workforce makes us a stronger and more innovative company. If you're passionate about making a difference, join us as we work together to build a brighter, more equitable, and sustainable future for all. Do you have what it takes?

title: Project P6 Scheduler – Project Management Office (PMO)
description: **Project P6 Scheduler** **Location:** Dublin, Cork, Galway. **Join Us In Shaping The Future Of Project Delivery** Join a leading team where your planning expertise will help shape the successful delivery of complex engineering and infrastructure projects. This is an excellent opportunity for an experienced Primavera P6 Scheduler to play a key role within a Project Management Office (PMO), supporting project managers and contributing directly to improved schedule performance, risk management, and project outcomes. **Purpose \& Scope Of The Role** We are seeking a Primavera P6 Scheduler to join the Project Management Office (PMO) and support Project Managers across a range of business sectors. The successful candidate will be responsible for assisting with the efficient delivery of projects and programmes, with particular focus on schedule management, risk, resourcing, and cost control. The role involves developing, maintaining, and analysing project schedules for multi\-disciplinary design projects from concept through to construction and handover. It also includes review and integration of contractor and supplier programmes into master project schedules, supporting reporting requirements, and helping to identify and implement corrective actions where required. **Your Impact In This Position** In this role, you will make a meaningful contribution to the successful delivery of projects by ensuring schedules are accurate, robust, and aligned with project objectives. You will help Project Managers maintain control of time, cost, and resources, while also supporting risk planning and analysis. Your work will directly improve visibility of project performance, strengthen decision\-making, and support the timely delivery of complex programmes. You will also contribute to continuous improvement by analysing project trends, capturing lessons learned, and supporting planning training and workshops. **Core Capabilities** **Planning \& Scheduling** Strong knowledge of project planning and scheduling principles, methods, and tools. Ability to develop, coordinate, track, and manage project schedules, identify critical issues, and support schedule performance across individual and portfolio\-level projects. **Attention to Detail** High level of accuracy in reviewing, interrogating, and maintaining schedules, cost data, and reporting outputs. Able to spot issues early and ensure information is reliable and actionable. **Communication \& Stakeholder Engagement** Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to work effectively with Project Managers, Clients, Contractors, Consultants, and internal stakeholders in a collaborative environment. **Problem Solving \& Initiative** Proactive approach to identifying schedule risks and issues, recommending corrective actions, and supporting timely resolution of complex planning challenges. **Teamwork \& Self\-Management** Works well as part of a multi\-disciplinary team, with strong organisational skills, good time management, and the ability to self\-motivate and deliver to deadlines. **Qualifications \& Experience** * Relevant degree or higher diploma in project management, civil engineering, or a related discipline. * Minimum 5\+ years’ experience in project planning, scheduling, and control across road and rail projects in Ireland, varying in size and complexity. * Experience working in an engineering design consultancy in the UK or Ireland as a Primavera P6 Scheduler. Experience should include logic development, developing and maintaining baselines, critical path mapping / analysis, cost and resource\-loading and adjustments including levelling, reporting metrics including earned value, e.g. SPI (Schedule Performance Index) and resource / cashflow profiling. * Proven knowledge of programme, risk, and resource management across engineering projects. * Experience developing Basis of Schedule documentation to support project schedules. * Strong report writing skills and the ability to present schedule information clearly and professionally. * Experience assisting with planning training and facilitating planning workshops is beneficial. * Experience with quantitative risk analysis techniques is advantageous. * Strong technical experience in delivery of projects within engineering consultancies and/or related construction projects. * Demonstrated experience working within accredited Integrated Management Systems (IMS) \& Standards, including quality, data security, and risk. * Proficient in MS Office Suite, particularly MS Excel and MS PowerPoint. **Working Environment** This role is based in Ireland and will involve working closely with Project Managers and multi\-disciplinary teams across a range of sectors. The position requires collaboration with internal stakeholders, Clients, Consultants, and Contractors. Flexibility to support project needs across different locations and working arrangements may be required, depending on project demands. Hybrid, remote, or flexible working arrangements can be incorporated where business and project requirements allow. **Why join Tetra Tech?** Tetra Tech is a leading provider of consulting and engineering services working across the full project lifecycle worldwide. We have a wide range of expertise across our teams, providing a global support network with a personalised approach to client relationships to better understand where we can add value. We provide a collaborative environment that supports individual performance, innovation, and creativity. W e support public and private sector clients on local, national, and international projects to deliver sustainable and resilient solutions. Our operations in the UK, Ireland, and the Netherlands include more than 6,000 employees who are Leading with Science® to solve our clients’ most complex problems. In alignment with the Equality Act 2010, we will make reasonable adjustments to support candidates and employees requiring additional arrangements. This could include adaptations to work schedules, training approaches, or the physical workspace. Please inform us if you need any accommodations during the recruitment process or in your day\-to\-day role. **Recruitment Agencies** We have a Preferred Supplier List of trusted partners to assist us when required and do not acknowledge any speculative CVs or unsolicited candidate introductions from agencies not on this list and any submitted will be taken as a gift. Please refer to the careers page on our website if you require any further information.

title: Qualified Financial Advisors
description: **Company Description** Protection \& Prosperity Financial Services Ltd ( **PPFS** ) is an established investment and insurance intermediary based in the North East of Ireland. We provide professional financial advice in life insurance, pensions, and investments to an established base of business and individual clients. Established in 2020, our company has grown significantly over the last five years. We have earned a reputation as a respected, progressive firm. Our mission is to help clients build their financial security by getting the fundamentals right, whether it's protecting their families, planning for retirement, or investing for the future. To support our continued expansion, we are seeking both experienced and aspiring qualified Financial Advisors to join our team. **Role Description** As a Financial Advisor with PPFS, you will primarily service an established portfolio of clients, ensuring their financial plans are regularly reviewed and kept up\-to\-date. You will manage existing client files with meticulous attention to compliance, maintaining records in line with financial regulations and company policies. Your remaining time will involve new business development – independently introducing new clients and opportunities to PPFS through networking and referrals, and contributing to our continued growth. We offer an appropriate package of benefits including: · A salary base commensurate to experience, · additional earning capacity tied to performance ( existing client retention and satisfaction, AUM Growth, new client development and team engagement ) · Paid courses / tuition for achievement of required credentials ( QFA, RPA, CFP, IOB, etc. ) Study Leave, regular training and upskilling ( technical, soft skills, regulatory compliance ) Mentorship \& coaching supports. · Death in Service Cover, Income Protection Benefits, Employee Assistance Programme. In addition to an employer funded Defined Contribution Pension Scheme · Provision of necessary equipment: Laptop, phone, software, CRM, compliance, with dedicated back\-office and administrative and compliance support You will be joining a team that values doing things the right way: all advice and sales processes must adhere to established regulatory and professional standard requirements, with a strong emphasis on ethics and compliance. You will have full back\-office support, including pre\-sales preparation, application processing, pipeline management, and compliance oversight. As an Investment and Insurance Intermediary, PPFS provides access to a broad range of life, pension, and investment products across the Irish market. This means you can offer truly tailored solutions from a wide array of providers to best meet your clients’ needs. Key Responsibilities: **Client Servicing:** Proactively manage and service an existing portfolio of clients, enhancing long\-term relationships and ensuring they receive the full benefits of the PPFS advisory process. Provide ongoing reviews and comprehensive financial planning across all relevant areas (protection, retirement planning, investments, etc.), with an emphasis on quality advice and excellent customer service. **Compliance \& File Management:** Ensure that all advice given and client documentation are maintained in strict compliance with Central Bank of Ireland regulations, the Consumer Protection Code, and internal policy standards. Maintain detailed records of recommendations and actions, upholding the highest professional standards in every case. **New Business Development:** Identify and pursue opportunities to generate new business, including leveraging existing client referrals and networking to introduce new customers to PPFS. Aiming to grow your portfolio while maintaining a high standard of service for your existing clients. **Performance \& Targets:** You will be responsible for the development and meeting of challenging performance targets and contributing to PPFS’s growth objectives. Using your initiative (with support from management when needed), you will drive results and continuously seek ways to expand and improve our client relationships. **Financial Solutions Expertise:** Provide holistic financial planning and advice tailored to each client’s needs. Our advisors offer guidance on a wide range of services including life insurance, income protection, company and personal pension schemes, retirement planning, savings \& investments, specified illness cover, and wealth management. You will stay up\-to\-date on product options and industry trends to deliver the best solutions for your clients. Ideal Candidate We are looking for a financial advisor who can hit the ground running and deliver exceptional service to our clients whilst working with an experienced team of advisors, administrators and management. The ideal candidate will have: **Experience:** Approximately 4\+ years of experience in the life insurance, pensions, and investments industry in Ireland, with exposure to pension schemes and wealth management advice considered a strong advantage. **Qualifications:** Full QFA (Qualified Financial Adviser) certification is required. You also meet all Central Bank of Ireland regulatory requirements for a financial advisor, including the Fitness \& Probity standards, the Minimum Competency Code, and you are committed to fulfilling your ongoing CPD obligations. **Client Focus \& Communication:** Excellent communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills are a must. You are client\-focused, able to build trust and rapport with a diverse range of customers, and dedicated to providing top\-tier service and advice at all times. **Compliance Mindset:** Strong knowledge of industry compliance standards and a disciplined approach to following regulatory guidelines in your daily work. You understand the importance of ethics, accuracy, and transparency in financial advising. **Technical Skills:** Proficiency in basic computer applications (Word, Excel, CRM systems) to manage client information and prepare reports efficiently. **Additional Requirements:** A full driving license and your own car, as the role may involve traveling to client meetings when necessary. How to Apply If you are ready to take the next step in your financial advisory career with a dynamic firm like PPFS, we’d love to hear from you. Please send your CV and a cover letter, in confidence, to: **Ciarán Shanley,** **Email:** ciaran@ppfs.ie **Phone:** 042 9359329 / 086 2580348 *All applications will be treated with the strictest confidentiality. Protection \& Prosperity Financial Services Ltd is regulated by the Central Bank of Ireland.*

title: Quality Analyst, Albanian Speaker - Video Safety Operations, T&S
description: **Responsibilities** About The Team Our QA team performs a critical function that supports our efforts to address objectionable or disturbing content. Content QA interacts with includes images, video, text related to every day life, but they can also include (but are not limited to) bullying; hate speech; child abuse; sexual assault; torture; bestiality; self\-harm; suicide; murder. It is possible that this role will be exosed to harmful content on a daily basis. Responsibilities \- Perform quality assessments by reviewing and evaluating the accuracy and consistency of policy application for content in the Albanian market. \- Analyse quality data to identify root causes, trends, and areas for improvement, summarizing findings in a clear and actionable way. \- Prepare and share QA reports with Content Quality Center stakeholders, acting as a point of alignment to ensure consistent quality standards across operations. \- Collaborate closely with Local Operations and Trust \& Safety teams, serving as an escalation point for policy\-related questions, issues, and solution alignment. \- Participate in and, when needed, facilitate coaching and calibration sessions to support consistent policy interpretation and application. **Qualifications** Minimum Qualifications \- At least 10 months of experience working with content quality, safety, or policy\-related activities. \- Strong understanding of European online safety, privacy, and freedom of expression topics. \- High attention to detail, with the ability to work independently and manage tasks with accuracy. \- Ability to thrive in a fast\-paced environment, adapting effectively to daily operational challenges. \- Resilience and a strong commitment to self\-care when handling emotionally demanding content. \- Flexibility to work different shifts, as required by the operation. \- High proficiency in the Albanian language, as this role supports the Albanian\-speaking market. Preferred Qualifications: \- Bachelor’s degree is preferred, however, not essential. \- Proven track record of working with co\-workers across different countries is preferable but not required. **Trust \& Safety** Content that this role interacts with includes images, video, and text related to every\-day life, but it can also include (but is not limited to) bullying; hate speech; child safety; depictions of harm to self and others, and harm to animals. Hence, it is possible that this role will be exposed to harmful content on a daily basis. ​ ​ TikTok recognises that keeping our platform safe for the TikTok communities is no ordinary job which can be both rewarding and psychologically demanding and emotionally taxing for some. This is why we are sharing the potential hazards, risks and implications in this unique line of work from the start, so our candidates are well informed before joining. ​ ​ We are committed to the wellbeing of all our employees and promise to provide comprehensive and evidence\-based programs, to promote and support physical and mental wellbeing throughout each employee's journey with us. We believe that wellbeing is a relationship and that everyone has a part to play, so we work in collaboration and consultation with our employees and across our functions in order to ensure a truly person\-centred, innovative and integrated approach.​ **About TikTok** TikTok is the leading destination for short\-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo.​ **Why Join Us** Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy \- a mission we work towards every day.​ We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.​ ​ Diversity \& Inclusion​ TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.​

title: Quality Assurance
description: Apply now » **Date:** May 13, 2026 **Location:** Rugauo City, CN, 226500 **Company:** Hubbell Incorporated **Job Overview** * 过程质量控制和巡检 * 根据巡检计划和路线，定期对设备、设施、环境等进行巡检； * 根据控制计划实施生产过程巡检，确保每个工序的产品符合设计和工艺要求； * 实施首件产品检验，按要求做好标识并记录数据，确保合格后方可批量生产； * 每4小时进行产品剖检，按要求拍摄剖检照片； * 如巡检过程中发现异常，应及时反馈质量工程师/专员及生产班长； * 产品护套厚度抽检和半成品外观全检 * 每班进行产品护套厚度抽检，识别偏芯的产品； * 每班对半成品（3台设备生产的90种产品）进行外观全检； * 检验报告 及时完成巡检日报表，护套厚度抽检及半成品外观全检纸质报告，并交由质量文员/工程师存档；同时完成电子文档数据登记，确保结果和数据真实、准确； * 不合格品管理 * 首件产品不合格，不可以批量生产，通知质量工程师/专员及生产班长； * 连续三模产品不合格，挂牌停机，通知质量工程师/专员及生产班长； * 不合格品需按要求标识隔离，转移到不合格品区； * 检具量具管理 负责检验巡检过程中检验工具和检测设备的管理，确保使用和运行正常； * 持续改进 * 协助质量工程师/专员跟踪品质改善措施的实施和验证； * 积极提报生产全过程中发现的异常问题点及改善方案； * 团队合作 积极配合其他同事完成相关工作内容； A Day In The Life Every Day At Hubbell Is Different And You’ll Contribute In Many Ways. On Any Given Day, You’ll Make a Difference By * Performing in\-process and final product acceptance based on AQL sampling. * Coordinating process improvement activities with work order; drawing changes with Mfg. Engineering and Operations as required. * Willing to assist or coordinate auditor efforts in other areas as required. * Keeping management apprised of progress and obstacles that constrain attainment of company objectives. * Understanding the plant policies and environment as it pertains to the safety of employees, operation of equipment, handling of equipment, and materials. * Understanding and monitoring QA and Production performance as defined in foundry control plans. What will help you thrive in this role? * High School Diploma * Associate’s or Bachelor’s Degree preferred * Prior Quality Assurance auditing experience (required) * Record of passing HPS QA Test (required) * Experience with SAP systems preferred * Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite **Job Segment:** Quality Assurance, SAP, ERP, Technology Apply now »

title: Quality Assurance Tester
description: **About the Role** Our Client is looking for **Quality Assurance Testers** to join their **LANGUAGE** team. In this role, you will execute test cases, analyze failures, file defects, and contribute to the continuous improvement of our testing processes. You will work within a defined test strategy and follow established Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) while collaborating with QA Engineers, QA Managers, and cross\-functional teams to deliver high\-quality customer experiences. You need to have QA testing experience and fluency in one or more non English languages as follows \- Spanish, Portuguese, German, French, Hindi, Arabic, Japanese If you have this experience we are interested in discussing this exciting role with you. **Key Responsibilities** Manual Testing * Executing test cases on websites, apps, or software systems * Checking whether features work as expected * Performing regression testing after fixes or updates * Testing across browsers, devices, or operating systems Language Validation * Use your language skills to review and complete **cultural and linguistic validation** \- ensure responses are accurate, natural, and appropriate for the target locale Bug Reporting * Identifying defects, errors, or usability issues * Logging bugs in systems like Jira, Azure DevOps, or TestRail * Writing clear reproduction steps * Verifying whether developers fixed reported issues Other * **Install and configure** software, tools, and test environments needed to execute tests. **Basic Qualifications** * **2\+ years** of experience in software quality assurance or testing. * Fluent proficiency in one or more **non\-English languages** (e.g., Spanish, Portuguese, German, French, Hindi, Arabic, Japanese). * Experience executing **manual and automated test cases** in a structured testing environment. * Experience in key tools like JIRA, Portman, Selenium would be desirable * **Software installation and configuration** experience across multiple platforms (Mac, Windows, Linux and Mobile devices) would be desirable. * Strong **attention to detail** and logical thinking **Additional Preferred Experience** * Basic understanding of software development lifecycle (SDLC) * familiarity with Agile/Scrum would be an advantage * Experience testing **voice assistants** , conversational AI, or NLU\-based products. * Familiarity with **automation frameworks** and the ability to run and review automated scripts (e.g., Python, Ruby or Shell scripts).

title: RAS Associate
description: We are Reckitt Home to the world's best loved and trusted hygiene, health, and nutrition brands. Our purpose defines why we exist: to protect, heal and nurture in the relentless pursuit of a cleaner, healthier world. We are a global team united by this purpose. Join us in our fight to make access to the highest quality hygiene, wellness, and nourishment a right and not a privilege. Research and Development In Research and Development, we're full of highly skilled talents that include Scientists, Engineers, Medical, Clinical and Regulatory professionals \- all working to create a cleaner, healthier world. With nine Centres of Excellence, we continually seek out new opportunities by using science, our entrepreneurial flare and our fearless innovation to develop and enhance our existing portfolio, never compromising on quality or performance. We do the right thing, always, by ensuring we act with responsibility and integrity, by complying with regulatory legislation across the globe, whilst ensuring our products are safe for our consumers and are to the highest quality. The size of our organisation means you'll have the opportunity to learn and work in different functions within R\&D, giving you exposure to different disciplines, teams and environments. You will also have access to our Research and Development Academy, designed to develop our team and allow you to grow in our great organisation. **About The Role** As Part of the Regulatory Team \- You will work in the Regulatory function, to lead and undertake all regulatory work necessary to meet the needs of the Reckitt business in Ireland as per defined priorities set by regulatory management, and in particular to be responsible for reviewing and submitting variations in compliance with all local regulations and Reckitt internal procedures. Your responsibilities * Regulatory compliance for Ireland Marketing Authorisations in Reckitts’s portfolio, including preparation and submission of variation packages and renewals to the Regulatory Agency * New submissions/eCTD/NeeS * Extensive CMC experience for Ireland submission of type I and type II variations (including grouping and work\-sharing), such as site transfers, change of manufacturing processes, FSP and analytical methods * Gap analysis of quality dossiers for licensing, including experience in reviewing and quality check of CMC documents for electronic submission to HPRA * Submission of type II safety variations to update SmPC and labelling in line with Company Core Data Sheets * Working with PV on PSUR submissions and SmPC updates * Creation and assessment of internal quality and non\-quality changes in line with the change control management system * Artwork review and approvals (PIL, primary and secondary packaging), including preparation of User Test Bridging Reports for the approval of leaflet changes * Work collaboratively with the relevant country teams to establish and implement the regulatory strategies within agreed timescales to bring competitive advantage to Reckitt brands * Support UKI Drug Safety Officer and relevant qualified employees to ensure compliance with pharmacovigilance and quality obligations * Interpret and apply all relevant legislation in Ireland to keep the Reckitt business up to date on national regulatory trends, risks and opportunities * Provide regulatory support and advice to other functions to ensure products are maintained in compliance with the local requirements The experience we're looking for * University Science degree in relevant scientific discipline and regulatory experience within Consumer Health or OTC industry or FMCG * Thorough understanding of the current IE and European regulations, laws, guidelines and industry requirements * Exceptional ability to manage workload and identify priority tasks to meet tight deadlines * Strong influencing / communication expertise * Ability to build good relationships and challenge constructively and confidently * Ability to consolidate large volumes of complex information, interpret \& construct straightforward plans. Naturally inquisitive and interested in our brands * Confident and capable of operating at all levels and able to resolve conflict situations * Competent with PC tools and information systems * Ability to challenge the status quo and propose improvement * Able to apply appropriate problem\-solving techniques to evaluate and resolve operational issues The skills for success Commercial Awareness, Business Partnership, Regulatory, Product Lifecycle Management, Propose improvement, Accountability, Adaptability, Business Strategy, Project \& Time Management, Collaboration \& Cross‑Functional Partnership, Detail Orientation \& Accuracy What we offer With inclusion at the heart of everything we do, working alongside our four global Employee Resource Groups, we support our people at every step of their career journey, helping them to succeed in their own individual way. We invest in the wellbeing of our people through parental benefits, an Employee Assistance Program to promote mental health, and life insurance for all employees globally. We have a range of other benefits in line with the local market. Through our global share plans we offer the opportunity to save and share in Reckitt's potential future successes. For eligible roles, we also offer short\-term incentives to recognise, appreciate and reward your work for delivering outstanding results. You will be rewarded in line with Reckitt's pay for performance philosophy. Equality We recognise that in real life, great people don't always 'tick all the boxes'. That's why we hire for potential as well as experience. Even if you don't meet every point on the job description, if this role and our company feels like a good fit for you, we still want to hear from you. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, disability or medical condition; colour, ethnicity, race, citizenship, and national origin; religion, faith; pregnancy, family status and caring responsibilities; sexual orientation; sex, gender identity, gender expression, and transgender identity; protected veteran status; size or any other basis protected by appropriate law.

title: Rack Decomissioning Technician , DCC Communities, DECOM
description: **Description** The Decommissioning Technical team is responsible for processing and facilitating large scale legacy Data Center equipment through a liquidation process to ensure that targeted equipment is ready to be removed off premises. Team members will also process large volumes of customer data to ensure that all customer data is sanitized and properly managed. This position requires candidates to be a self\-starter, highly motivated individual who can adhere to strict processes and be able to perform repetitive tasks with no deviation and demonstrate continuously consistent results. Team Members will be working in a team environment not only with other members of the team but also from other teams and must be able to communicate and promote cross\-team functionality. This is an entry level position and qualified candidates do not require intimate details of software operations or networking protocols. Amazon Data Centers takes customer data with the utmost importance, and selected candidates must be able to perform repetitive tasks without variance and adhere to strict Standard Operating Procedures (SOP) that ensures customer data is protected and properly handled. Key job responsibilities **Data Center Technicians (Decommission Technician) Will Be Responsible For** Decommissioning and processing of legacy hardware out of the data center facilities Moving/lifting packages usually no greater than 75 lbs. Unpacking/repacking fragile electronics Relocating and moving of large enterprise equipment Creating/attaching labels and barcodes Hard drive installation and removal, and destruction Basic Networking removal/installation and clearing of configurations. Physical layer and Application layer Updating progress through a web ticketing system Reporting of processes and regular status updates Documenting procedures Escalating to senior technicians and management as needed/required **Familiarity With Basic PC Components/hardware** * HDD/SSD/Flash/Removable storage media * USB/FireWire/Esata * Ram/CPU/Motherboard/Input\-Output devices There may be an element of shift based work required with this role. A day in the life AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help. You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion. **About The Team** About AWS Diverse Experiences AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying. Why AWS? Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses. Inclusive Team Culture AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee\-led and company\-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do. Mentorship \& Career Growth We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge\-sharing, mentorship and other career\-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better\-rounded professional. Work/Life Balance We value work\-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve. **Basic Qualifications** * High school or equivalent diploma * Experience with computer hardware troubleshooting and repair * Experience in networking **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience dealing effectively with customers during problem resolution and operating efficiently under pressure Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates. Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status. Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. title: Real Estate Credit Underwriting Analyst / Graduate
description: **Company Overview** KKR is a leading global investment firm that offers alternative asset management as well as capital markets and insurance solutions. KKR aims to generate attractive investment returns by following a patient and disciplined investment approach, employing world\-class people, and supporting growth in its portfolio companies and communities. KKR sponsors investment funds that invest in private equity, credit and real assets and has strategic partners that manage hedge funds. KKR’s insurance subsidiaries offer retirement, life and reinsurance products under the management of Global Atlantic Financial Group. References to KKR’s investments may include the activities of its sponsored funds and insurance subsidiaries. **Kkr Real Estate Overview** KKR Real Estate was founded in 2011 on the belief that a dedicated real estate team with to KKR’s global investment platform could create differentiated deal flow and compelling risk\-adjusted returns. The primary mandate of KKR Real Estate is to leverage the KKR brand, scale across credit and equity and integrate within the larger KKR investing ecosystem to deliver a differentiated investing opportunity. KKR Real Estate team is made up of access approximately 140 real estate investment and asset management professionals and has grown to over $85 billion of AUM. Its capabilities across real estate equity, real estate loans, and real estate securities, under the same leadership team, has allowed it to create a best\-in\-class and global portfolio. **Kkr Real Estate Credit \& K\-star Investment Services Overview** KKR Real Estate Credit was formed in January 2015 to invest in real estate credit products and launched the European Real Estate Credit business in 2022\. KKR Real Estate Credit currently invests through 8 separately capitalized vehicles across our Direct Lending and Securities portfolios and consists of 120\+ dedicated professionals based in New York, San Francisco, Houston, Dallas, London and Dublin. In 2022, KKR created K\-Star Asset Management LLC (“K\-Star”), a dedicated investment services platform based in Dallas, Texas. The K\-Star team includes industry specialists overseeing asset management, loan origination, securities investing, risk management and client experience for KKR’s global real estate credit franchise. In 2024, K\-Star established an office in Dublin, supporting KKR’s real estate credit portfolio across Western Europe. **Position Overview** K\-Star is seeking an underwriting professional to join its dedicated Real Estate Credit team in Dublin, Ireland. The position will support *the analysis and evaluation of potential direct lending opportunities* in EMEA and help drive KKR Real Estate Credit’s continued growth in the market. The primary responsibilities for the role includes modelling and underwriting commercial loans across multiple asset classes, geographies, and KKR investment vehicles, managing the entire due diligence process (both internally and externally), and preparing investment memorandums. **Responsibilities** In addition to the above, the successful candidate will also: * Actively coordinate investment process (bidding, due diligence, and closing) * Participate in due diligence sessions, loan structuring, and closing processes * Participate in pipeline meetings, deal screening sessions, and investment committees * Develop investment themes and conduct macro and micro level data analysis to inform investment decisions * Assist team with ongoing loan asset management and reporting requirements. * Work collaboratively with investment professionals in KKR Real Estate Credit and KKR Real Estate Equity as well as across the broader KKR organization. **Qualifications** * Bachelor’s degree required * Recent Graduate. 0 – 3 years of relevant experience, preferably in commercial real estate lending or a closely related area * Superior analytical and financial modelling skills with demonstrated investment acumen, knowledge and thoughtfulness as a credit\-minded investor * Strong knowledge of commercial real estate, fixed income, and financial concepts * Exceptional written and presentation skills and strong executive presence In addition to the above, we are looking for someone that is: * Collaborative and has strong relationship building skills * Able to work independently and represent KKR and K\-Star’s culture and values in a professional manner * Goal\-oriented and has the ability to effectively multi\-task, prioritize, and manage timelines * Entrepreneurial, resourceful, and a self\-starter * Intellectually curious and has a desire to continuously expand their knowledgebase * Committed to the highest level of integrity and professionalism KKR is an equal opportunity employer. Individuals seeking employment are considered without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex, marital status, ancestry, physical or mental disability, veteran status, sexual orientation, or any other category protected by applicable law. KKR will provide reasonable accommodations as required by applicable federal, state, and/or local laws. Individuals seeking an accommodation for the application or interview process should email Benefits@kkr.com. Emails sent for unrelated issues, such as following up on an application, will not receive a response. If you are a qualified individual with a disability or a disabled veteran, you may request a reasonable accommodation if you are unable or limited in your ability to use or access https://www.kkr.com/careers because of your disability. You can request reasonable accommodations by sending an email to Benefits@kkr.com. Only emails left for this purpose will be returned. **Massachusetts Applicants:** It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability. This notice applies only to applicants and employees who work or will work in Massachusetts, in accordance with applicable state law.

title: Reception & Student Experience
description: **Job Description** Are you a naturally confident and friendly person? Do you love Dublin and love to show it off to visiting friends and international tourists? Are you looking for full\-time work? ELI Schools is a fast\-growing English language training provider and we're searching for a full\-time Student Experience to help deliver a range of exciting events for adult international students. We are searching for someone to deliver a fun and entertaining calendar of social and cultural activities ranging from city walking tours to museum trips and weekend tours to the best places in Ireland. The role requires someone with a flexible schedule, who has availability to work weekday. The role also requires at least one evening event and two one day weekend tours per month. Our adult students are aged 22 – 30 and arrive in Ireland on a gap\-year type experience, ready to enjoy the best that Ireland can offer. The majority are here on long\-term programs eager to find part\-time work and to enjoy living and working in our beautiful country. We are searching for someone who is outgoing, naturally friendly, and has the confidence to speak and get to know new people. Your role will be to provide a balance of activities for students who have just arrived, and those that are here longer term and are looking for social and cultural things to do. The role might suit a recent graduate or current university student who has flexible timetable. Requirements: * Flexibility to work at weekday, some evenings and weekends * Naturally confident and ability to interact with people in public * Fluent English Speaker (C1\) * Second language like Spanish is desirable Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Schedule: * Day shift * Monday to Friday * Weekend availability Ability to commute/relocate: * Dublin 2, CO. Dublin: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Language: * English (required) Work Location: In person Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Ability to commute/relocate: * Dublin, County Dublin: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Language: * English (required) Work Location: In person Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €28,000\.00\-€30,000\.00 per year Ability to commute/relocate: * Dublin, County Dublin: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Language: * English (required) Work Location: In person

title: Regulatory Oversight Associate
description: **About Us** At Davy, it's the unique talents of all our people that have been the foundations of our success for 100 years. As we continue to grow, so do you. Because you are not just part of our team \- you are a key player in shaping our future. **At Davy, you are the difference.** Established in 1926, the Davy Group sits at the heart of wealth and capital in Ireland, providing a broad range of services to high\-net\-worth individuals, self\-directed investors, small\-to\-medium enterprises, credit unions, corporations, and domestic \& international institutional investors. Our culture is shaped around three values, which are at the heart of everything we do: client success, one Davy, and building a proud legacy. Sharing the same goals, we work together to deliver exceptional outcomes for our clients. As part of a team of over 900, you will be encouraged to learn and to grow, both professionally and personally. **About The Role** An exciting opportunity has opened up in our Davy First Line of Defence team with Wealth Risk. In the role, the successful candidate will: * Maintain and manage the First Line of Defence risk register including logging new risk events, breaches, complaints and updating as applicable, and following up with team members to ensure closure of events. * Perform quality assurance checks on risk data submissions from business units to ensure completeness and accuracy before reporting. * Overseeing the database management for the First Line of Defence Risk team, compiling and analysing data, and generating reports. * Conduct local review of risk events, perform root\-cause analysis of events, and follow up with the business with queries and resolution. * Prepare monthly risk reports and department MI for Management team. * Support regulatory, internal audit, and compliance requests, including preparing data extracts, gathering evidence, and coordinating responses with business teams. * Provide day‑to‑day support to business units on risk queries, acting as a point of contact for guidance on risk processes and standards. * Maintain 1LOD risk documentation. **Requirements** **About you** * Strong educational background (third level relevant degree and/or professional qualification). * Minimum of 2 years working in Financial Services industry. * Comfort navigating large data sets, compiling data analyses, displaying data effectively and communicating results with impact. * Proficiency with Excel, PowerPoint, PowerBi and Word. * Excellent communication skills, both verbal and written. * Excellent attention to detail. * Have excellent organisational skills with an ability to manage, prioritise, work under pressure, and meet tight deadlines. * Inquisitive, self\-starter who can work autonomously as well as part of a team. **Benefits** **What we offer** At Davy, our people are our biggest investment. In today's fast\-moving world, we know it's important to feel secure and empowered. That's why Davy benefits support you through all of life's stages, offering health and wellness, flexible working options, flexible benefits allowance, learning and development opportunities, through to assisted retirement planning. **Health and Wellbeing** Psychological safety, emotional wellbeing and support are a priority at Davy. We offer a comprehensive Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) and mental health first aid. Our Sports \& Social teams promote physical wellbeing and social connection. **Reward and Recognition** We offer a competitive remuneration package to include salary, bonus, pension and a host of additional benefits to protect you and your family when it matters most. **Growth and Development** Our award\-winning L\&D function supports you and your growth at Davy. We offer a comprehensive range of professional and personal development opportunities. Our culture supports learning, and we encourage you to explore more at Davy. **Positive Workplace** Our workplace is inclusive, diverse, and offers everyone a sense of belonging. We are flexible and continue to support a hybrid working model. You will get to be your best at Davy. **Values and Culture** Exceptional client outcomes delivered by a world class team, underpinned by our core Davy values \- One Team, Client Success, and Proud Legacy. **Important Information** Davy is an equal opportunities employer, committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace. We value diversity in both background and experience, and even if you don't meet all of the requirements outlined in the job description, we still encourage you to apply. We review all applications received. As part of our commitment to creating an accessible environment, we want to ensure that everyone has an equal opportunity to participate in the interview process. If you require any reasonable accommodations, please let us know, and we will do our best to make the necessary arrangements. Appointment to this role is subject to the candidate's eligibility to work in Ireland. Where agency assistance is required, our Recruitment Team will engage directly with suppliers. Unsolicited CVs / profiles supplied to us by recruitment agencies will not be accepted for this role. Davy Group is a member of the Bank of Ireland Group.
